name: CatBoost (Gradient Boosting with Categorical Features)

performance.notes: GPU training support; inference sub-millisecond for tabular data

agent_readiness.notes: Gradient boosting optimized for categorical features. CatBoostClassifier(iterations=500, cat_features=['col1', 'col2']). Automatic handling of categorical features (no need to encode). Symmetric trees for faster prediction. Ordered boosting to reduce overfitting. sklearn compatible fit/predict. GPU training. Feature importance. SHAP values. Prediction uncertainty. R interface. Competitive with XGBoost/LightGBM especially when data has many categorical features. By Yandex.

gotchas: Slower training than LightGBM for pure numerical data
cat_features must be specified (or be string dtype) — easy to forget
Default hyperparameters may need tuning for small datasets
best_when: Tabular data with many categorical columns
Avoiding one-hot encoding / label encoding preprocessing
Competition ML where categorical handling matters
When XGBoost overfits and you want ordered boosting
avoid_when: Deployment where model size is critical
Very large datasets where LightGBM's histogram algorithm is faster
summary: CatBoost is Yandex's gradient boosting library with native categorical feature support. Specify cat_features=['col'] and CatBoost handles encoding automatically. Symmetric trees, ordered boosting. GPU training. sklearn compatible. SHAP values and feature importance built in. Competitive with XGBoost/LightGBM for high-cardinality categorical data. LightGBM faster for pure numerical.
